title: The heuristics for what to do now
html:
Most of the time, the answer is already in you. Deep down you know what’s right to do — the honest next move, the thing you keep circling back to. You just don’t usually listen, because listening means admitting the answer is inconvenient. So when you genuinely don’t know what to do now, stop interrogating your to-do list and instead listen for:

  • Things that you feel the resistance, that you always avoided. The friction itself is the signal — we rarely flinch away from what doesn’t matter.
  • Things that break your balance, put you in the scarcity mentality. The moves that make you feel like there isn’t enough are usually the ones forcing you to grow past your current size.
  • Things that you dream of but don’t believe you can reach. The quiet ambition you dismiss as unrealistic is often the most accurate map of where you actually want to go.

Notice that all three point toward discomfort, not away from it. That is not a coincidence; it is the method. Think of someone who has been meaning to start writing for years but keeps “not finding the time” — the resistance, the fear it won’t be good enough, the half-buried dream are all pointing in the same direction. Those are the North of your compass. (A heuristic I borrowed from a friend’s notes.)

  • Feel the Sensation
